results.title: 5 OBS Alternatives for Recording Meetings That Won't Kill Your Machine (2026)
results.content:
Its quintessential Studio Mode provides a preview and program output display. This allows you to make changes to scenes and sources without affecting the live stream or recording, ensuring smooth transitions. There are various plug-ins and extensions that work with OBS Studio, offering to enhance functionality even further.

In terms of drawbacks, OBS Studio suffers the same fate of all OBS softwares. That’s why we’ve compiled a list of 5 OBS alternatives that can be used specifically to record meetings and not destroy your computer in the process.

## 5 OBS Alternatives for Recording Meetings

### 1. tl;dv

tl;dv is the best OBS alternative when it comes to recording meetings. If your goal with OBS is to record your virtual calls, then tl;dv will save you time and machine resources. [...] Note: If you are looking for an AI-powered video and audio editing tool with advanced transcription and screen recording capabilities, consider Descript. It offers text-based video editing, filler word removal, and AI audio enhancement.

Bandicam allows you to record your entire screen, or just a single window. It’s a broader scope OBS alternative that can still be used to record meetings. [...] Camtasia allows you to record everything happening on your screen, including presentations, slideshows, document sharing, and discussions, even during meetings. Like most OBS competitors on this list, Camtasia empowers you to record your screen, your audio, and your webcam. It has a built-in integration with PowerPoint so you can import presentations and record them during your call. This is great if you’re presenting something important and want to practice beforehand.

results.title: Best Software to Record Meetings in 2026
results.content:
This article breaks down your options, from native tools built into your video conferencing platform to smarter alternatives that skip the video file entirely.

Here's what you'll find inside:

 Native recording options in Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams
 Third-party recording software like Otter.ai, Fathom, and Fireflies
 Why transcription often beats recording for most use cases
 How Tactiq captures meetings without bots or bulky video files
 Key features to look for before choosing a tool

## Native Meeting Recording Options

Most video conferencing tools come with built-in recording features. Before reaching for a third-party tool, it's worth knowing what your current platform already offers, and where it falls short.

### How to record Zoom meetings [...] Tactiq is built exactly for this. It transcribes your meetings in real time inside Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams. No bot, no recording, no bulky video files. You get accurate meeting transcripts, AI-powered summaries, and searchable records of all your meetings the moment the call ends.

Tactiq_ The Transcription Alternative to Meeting Recording Software

Tactiq\_ The Transcription Alternative to Meeting Recording Software

Install the free Tactiq Chrome extension and have your first meeting transcribed in minutes.

{{rt\_cta\_ai-convenience}}

## Key Features to Look for in Meeting Recording Software

Not all meeting recording software is built the same. Here's what to evaluate before choosing a tool. [...] ### Do I need permission to record a meeting?

Yes. Most regions require at least one-party consent, and many require all participants to be notified. Always inform meeting participants before recording.

### Can I record a meeting without the host knowing?

This depends on the platform and local laws. In most cases, recording without consent is not permitted. Tactiq transcribes meetings without a recording. Only you see the transcript unless you choose to share it.

‍

results.title: 10 Best Free Meeting Recorder Apps 2026 (Transcribe, No Bot) | Meetily Blog
results.content:
The best meeting recording app for most people in 2026 is Meetily: it records Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, Discord, and Webex calls locally on Windows or macOS, adds no bot to the meeting, and is free under MIT license. It bundles AI transcription (Whisper or Parakeet) and AI summaries in the same app, so you do not need a separate transcriber. For sales-heavy workflows with CRM dependency, Fireflies.ai is the conventional pick despite its visible bot. For unlimited free cloud recording, Fathom is the alternative. None of the other apps in this guide combine bot-free, local, and free in one package. [...] For free recording plus AI transcription in one app, Meetily Community Edition is the strongest pick in 2026: it captures system audio locally (no bot in the call), transcribes with OpenAI Whisper across 99+ languages, and stores everything on-device under MIT license. Fathom Free is the strongest cloud alternative - unlimited transcription, but the bot joins meetings and audio uploads to Fathom's US servers. OBS Studio records audio but does not transcribe. For privacy-sensitive work (healthcare, legal, financial), Meetily is the only free combination that keeps audio off vendor servers. [...] GDPR and Compliance Warning

For European businesses, sending meeting recordings to US-based cloud services can create GDPR compliance issues. Otter.ai, Fireflies.ai, and Fathom all process data on US servers. Local recording with tools like Meetily eliminates this risk entirely. See our GDPR compliance guide for more details.

## Why Is "OBS Alternative for Meetings" a Top Search?

OBS Studio is powerful, but it wasn't designed as meeting recording software. Here's why people search for OBS alternatives:

results.content:
‍

Tactiq doesn't record your meetings; it does something more useful. It captures a full, real-time transcript of every conversation across Zoom, Google Meet, and Microsoft Teams, then uses AI to turn that transcript into summaries, action items, and automated workflows. No audio files to store, no video to scrub through, and no bot joining the call. Just the information you actually need, ready when the meeting ends.

### What you'll like about Tactiq [...] ## ‍

## 8. SMRecorder

SMRecorder

SMRecorder

SMRecorder is a free Windows screen recorder that captures audio and video directly from your computer. It is a basic tool with no AI features, suited for simple screen capture rather than dedicated online meeting recording.

### What you'll like about SMRecorder

 Free to use: No cost, no subscription required.
 Integrated audio recording: Captures both system audio and microphone simultaneously.
 Video file formats: Saves recordings as MP4 or AVI files.

### What you might not like

 Windows only; not available on macOS or mobile devices
 No transcription, summaries, or AI capabilities
 Not designed specifically for online meeting recording
 Last updated in 2018; limited ongoing development

Pricing: Free

## 9. Apple Screen Recorder [...] ### What you might not like

 Not built for live meeting recording or real-time transcription
 Storage limits apply to the free plan
 Billing changes following the Atlassian acquisition have caught some users off guard

Pricing (monthly):

 Starter: Free - up to 25 videos, 5-minute recording limit
 Business: $18/user/month - unlimited videos, 4K quality
 Business + AI: $24/user/month - auto-summaries, AI editing, transcript-based editing

## 7. Veed

veed

veed

Veed is a browser-based video editor with basic screen recording capabilities. It works well for polishing meeting recordings, but it is not purpose-built for live meeting transcription or AI note-taking.

### What you'll like about Veed

results.title: 9 Best AI Note Takers for Meetings (2026): Ranked & Reviewed
results.content:
The truth of the matter is a lot of work gets done outside of formal recorded meetings, like on Slack, or email and Littlebird is one of the only tools that captures all of that context.

The tradeoff here is that while you do get a smarter, more useful note-taking experience, the context awareness layer means it's more invasive. With other tools like Granola, we feel comfortable using everywhere, even in our private lives, but the level of access Littlebird requires gives us pause. That doesn't make it unusable since they have controls that give you some guardrails, but it is something to consider.
